label: South Shore Lake Effect Snow Forecast

comments.text: Happy Halloween. Much of the country will have good trick or treating weather this evening through tonight. The exceptions will be for areas of the Pacific Northwest, from the Upper Midwest into the Great Lakes, and across the northern and central Appalachians.

Happy Sunday afternoon from the Northland! It's a mild afternoon for those that are seeing plentiful sunshine. Others are dealing with a cool spring day with clouds and a breeze. The South Shore snowbelt will need to ready for a snowy overnight.

Happy #Sunday afternoon from the #Northland! It's a #mild afternoon for those that are seeing plentiful #sunshine. Others are dealing with a #cool #spring day with #clouds and a breeze. The #SouthShore snowbelt will need to ready for lake effect #snow […] [Original post on newsie.social]

7:15 P.M. WEATHER UPDATE: #Showers and a few weak #thunderstorms are beginning to move into the #Northland this evening. They will become widespread over the next several hours. Dense #fog is also being reported in the #TwinPorts region and near #LakeSuperior […] [Original post on newsie.social]

LAKE EFFECT ON SOUTH SHORE ALERT: Once winds shift to the northwest and north Friday, the South Shore region of the Northland will see heavy lake effect snow showers form and come on shore through Saturday. Snowfall totals will be impressive, with difficult driving conditions likely.

A little taste of the snow is coming along the Twin Ports region early this afternoon. An east wind is bringing a band of lake effect snow to this region. Let's just say your neighborhood friendly meteorologist and wants to visually show you to be READY FOR SNOW NORTHLAND!

REACP OF WET & WINDY STORM: The past 24 hours have played out as forecasted in the Northland. The North Shore saw the highest rainfall totals, but everyone got at least around 0.50". Winds were also gusty, with the highest totals near Lake Superior.

HOW MUCH SNOW & HOW MUCH MORE?: As forecasted, the northern areas of the Northland saw the most snowfall since Sunday, with those north of Highway 2 seeing some accumulation. Lake effect showers resume tonight along the South Shore snowbelt region. Expect a couple inches.

HOW MUCH SNOW & HOW MUCH MORE?: As forecasted, the northern areas of the #Northland saw the highest #snowfall totals since Sunday, with those north of Highway 2 seeing some #accumulation. #Lake #effect #snow #showers resume tonight along the #SouthShore […] [Original post on newsie.social]

Happy Thursday afternoon from the Northland! It's another cool day with wind chill readings below zero at times. There isn't much sunshine, and nearly all snow showers are taking place along the South Shore.

There are several weather alerts in effect for parts of the Northland. Overall, the windy conditions are worst in the Twin Ports region, both on land and on Lake Superior. Where it isn't raining, fire concerns are very high to extreme.

LATEST ON NEXT STORM SYSTEM: After a calm end to March, April will start off #stormy for the #Northland as a strong #strom system will push through the #GreatLakes region. Heavy #snow #showers will be the main focus Tuesday night for everyone. Then we have to […] [Original post on newsie.social]

THURSDAY'S CLIPPER: Thanks to fluffier snowflakes and lake enhancement, the clipper system coming through the Northland will have more snowfall that usual systems like this. Highest totals are likely from Silver Bay southeast through Moose Lake, and the Bayfield Peninsula.

THURSDAY'S CLIPPER: Thanks to fluffier #snowflakes and lake enhancement, the #clipper system coming through the #Northland Thursday will have more #snowfall that usual systems like this. Highest #snow totals of 6 to 9 inches are likely from the southern half […] [Original post on newsie.social]

Happy Wednesday afternoon from the Northland! The latest spring storm system passing through the Great Lakes doesn't want to bother us with nearly all of its snow showers. All we are seeing are cloudy skies, seasonally cool temperatures, and a strong breeze.

Happy #Wednesday afternoon from the #Northland! The latest spring #storm system passing through the Great Lakes doesn't want to bother us with nearly all of its #snow #showers. All we are seeing are #cloudy skies, seasonally #cool #temperatures, and a strong […] [Original post on newsie.social]

Everyone in the Northland will deal with the strong winds from a storm system pushing through the Great Lakes region tonight, with heavy snowfall for northwest Wisconsin and the Upper Peninsula. Conditions improve Monday, but it stays cool. VIDEO: www.fox21online.com/2025/12/28/s...

LAKE EFFECT ON SOUTH SHORE ALERT: Once #winds shift to the northwest and north Friday, the #SouthShore region of the #Northland will see heavy #lakeeffect #snow #showers form and come on shore through Saturday. #Snowfall totals will be impressive, with […] [Original post on newsie.social]

Happy Thursday afternoon from the Northland! The initial burst of snowfall from a storm system approaching the Great Lakes region is passing through. After a break, we will see more snow showers and warming temperatures.
The two British guys experiencing South Haven, MI weather, food, and Lake Michigan for the first time on Instagram (and probably TT, but I don't fuck with that) are giving me joy this week.

SOUTH SHORE LAKE EFFECT: As #snow #showers wrap up across most of the #Northland after tonight, a persistent northwest #breeze will keep lake effect snow showers ongoing along the South Shore region. Some spots in the #UpperPeninsula could receive 12 to 20 […] [Original post on newsie.social]

SOUTH SHORE LAKE EFFECT: As snow showers wrap up across the Northland after tonight, a persistent northwest wind will keep lake effect snow showers ongoing along the South Shore region. Some spots in the Upper Peninsula could receive 12 to 20 inches by the end of the weekend.

HIGH WAVES WITH NEXT STORM SYSTEM: The #windy conditions from the next #storm system will also create some decent #waves on #LakeSuperior and along the shorelines of the #Northland. The farther you go north along the #NorthShore, the better chances of seeing […] [Original post on newsie.social]

WINDY SUNDAY NIGHT: A strengthening low-pressure system will pass near the #Northland tonight, creating strong #winds through Monday morning. Locations near #LakeSuperior will see gusts up to 50 mph. Seems fitting for the anniversary of the […] [Original post on newsie.social]

HOW WINDY & SNOWY WAS THIS WEEK: Wednesday was windy across the Northland. It isn't often that we see gusts over 40 mph away from Lake Superior. It's also been snowy in the Arrowhead and South Shore snowbelt regions this week.

Happy Wednesday afternoon Northland! Snow showers continue to fall across the region, and that is leading to winter road conditions and reduced visibility. Lake effect snow showers will continue along the South Shore through tonight.

Happy #Wednesday afternoon from the #Northland! Scattered #snow #showers continue to fall across the region, and that is leading to #winter road conditions and reduced #visibility. #Lakeeffect snow showers will continue along the #SouthShore through tonight […] [Original post on newsie.social]

SOUTH SHORE LAKE EFFECT RETURNS: The strong northwest winds Wednesday will also create heavy lake effect snow showers along the South Shore snowbelt region. Up to 10 inches could fall near the shorelines in Gogebic, Iron, and Ontonagon counties.

SOUTH SHORE LAKE EFFECT RETURNS: The #strong northwest #winds Wednesday in the #Northland will also create heavy #lake #effect #snow #showers along the South Shore snowbelt region. Up to 10 inches of #snowfall could occur near the shorelines in Gogebic, Iron […] [Original post on newsie.social]

Happy Tuesday afternoon from the Northland! It is a warmer day, with lighter winds making it feel not as cold as Monday. The sunshine is persistent for most of us, but so are the clouds in the Arrowhead and South Shore snowbelt regions.

SNOWFALL FOR SOME, WINDY FOR ALL: A clipper system will bring snow showers across the Northland tonight and Friday. The highest snowfall totals will be in the Arrowhead and South Shore snowbelt regions. Everyone will have to deal with strong winds as temperatures cool throughout Friday.

SNOWFALL FOR SOME, WINDY FOR ALL: A #clipper system will bring #snow #showers across the #Northland Thursday tonight and Friday. The highest #snowfall totals will be in the Arrowhead and South Shore snowbelt regions. Everyone will have to deal with strong […] [Original post on newsie.social]

WEEKEND SNOWFALL TOTALS: The snow showers since Valentine's Day have contained big and fluffy snowflakes for the Northland. Thanks to northeast winds creating lake effect, and some spots saw 9 to 12 inches. More snow showers continue for the Upper Peninsula through Monday.

WEEKEND SNOWFALL TOTALS: The #snow #showers across the #Northland since Valentine's Day have contained big and fluffy #snowflakes. Thanks to a northeast #breeze creating #lakeeffect bands, and you get some decent #snowfall totals. Some spots saw 9 to 12 […] [Original post on newsie.social]

MORE SNOW COMING: The Northland will see a couple more storm systems pass through early this week that will bring more chances of snow showers. The first will be for the northern half tonight and Monday, with the Rainy River Basin likely seeing a couple inches.

MORE SNOW COMING: The #Northland will see a couple more #storm systems pass through early this week that will bring more chances of #snow #showers. The first will be for the northern half tonight and Monday, with the Rainy River Basin likely seeing a couple […] [Original post on newsie.social]

Happy Monday afternoon from the Northland! The winds are strong and the temperatures are cooler when compared to the weekend. Showers are scattered about. They will continue through tonight and become all snow. Some areas near Lake Superior could see accumulation.

Happy #Sunday afternoon from the #Northland! It's a #warm end to the weekend for some of us with peeks of #sunshine. Others are having to dodge #rain and a stray #thunderstorm as a #storm system passes through the western #GreatLakes region. More widespread […] [Original post on newsie.social]

4:45 P.M. RADAR UPDATE: The heavy snowfall has reached the Arrowhead and North Shore regions of the Northland. It has exited the Twin Ports. Winds remain strong, with highest gusts along Cook and Lake counties. Blizzard Warning remains in effect along the Lake Superior shoreline.

There are several #weather alerts in effect for parts of the #Northland through Tuesday night. Overall, the #windy conditions are worst in the #TwinPorts region, both on land and on #LakeSuperior. Where it isn't #raining, #fire concerns are back to very high […] [Original post on newsie.social]

The biggest issue for the Northland are the strong winds, with gusts over 30 mph. They will be continuing through Tuesday night. This will lead to high waves near the Lake Superior shoreline, as well as new extreme fire danger levels for some locations on Tuesday.

The biggest #weather issue for the #Northland are the strong #winds, with #gusts over 30 mph. These conditions will continuing through Tuesday night. This will lead to high #waves on #Lake Superior, as well as new extreme #fire danger levels for some locations […] [Original post on newsie.social]

8 P.M. #RADAR UPDATE: A scattered line of #showers and #thunderstorms continue to push through the Northland late this evening. Some have strengthened due to interacting with the lake #breeze, but they are not strong or severe. More scattered showers and […] [Original post on newsie.social]

Happy #Thursday afternoon from the #Northland! The #fog has dissipated across the region, but it will remain an issue next to #LakeSuperior, with some of it coming on shore tonight and Friday morning. It remains #warm and #dry, but #showers and #thundertorms […] [Original post on newsie.social]

As #forecasted, the South Shore and Upper Peninsula regions have seen the most #snowfall in the #Northland, with more snow falling the rest of today and tonight. #Winds have been #strong, with #gusts of 40+ mph last night and Wednesday morning. They will […] [Original post on newsie.social]

LATEST ON UPCOMING WINTER STORM SYSTEM: The Northland remains on track to seeing its first widespread winter weather event of the season Tuesday and Wednesday. The worst conditions will be passing through Tuesday evening & overnight. The most snowfall will be along the South Shore.

Happy Tuesday afternoon from the Northland! A passing clipper system continues to bring some of us snow showers through the rest of today. The focus will be around Lake Superior. It is a warmer day compared to yesterday, but it will cool down again soon.

Happy Sunday afternoon from the Northland! The worst of the arctic cold is passed us. We are going to see a warm up the next several days. Some light snow showers are passing through the rest of today, mainly north of Highway 2 and around Lake Superior.

Lake effect snow showers will persist along the South Shore Snowbelt region through tonight, leading to a couple inches of new snowfall. The rest of the Northland will see gusty winds and dropping temperatures. Wind chills will be below zero by Wednesday morning.

#Lakeeffect #snow #showers will persist along the South Shore Snowbelt region through Tuesday night, leading to a couple inches of new #snowfall. The rest of the #Northland will see #gusty #winds and dropping #temperatures. #Windchills will be below zero by […] [Original post on newsie.social]

MESSY CLIPPER SYSTEM: The #Northland will have to deal with a mix of #snow, #sleet, #freezing #rain, and freezing #drizzle Monday night and Tuesday. Most of us will see a bit of snow and #ice accumulation. The South Shore Snowbelt region will see lake effect […] [Original post on newsie.social]

MESSY CLIPPER SYSTEM: The Northland will have to deal with a mix of snow, sleet, freezing rain, and freezing drizzle Monday night and Tuesday. Most of us will see a bit of snow and ice accumulation. The South Shore Snowbelt region will see lake effect snow showers, increasing their snowfall.

CLIPPER SYSTEM TONIGHT & TUESDAY: The Northland will see a quick round of snow showers tonight and Tuesday. The Arrowhead will see the highest snowfall totals, followed by the South Shore Snowbelt. A strong breeze will also make travel difficult in spots.

7 P.M. RADAR UPDATE: The severe weather threat in the Northland has passed as the line of scattered showers and thunderstorms is in the marine layer of air near Lake Superior. Some moderate rainfall and gusty winds are still possible for the North Shore this hour.

Happy Thursday afternoon from the Northland! The first of two winter storm systems continues to push through the region, keeping temperatures cool and winds strong off Lake Superior. Most of us are seeing snow, but some sleet and freezing rain is falling south of Highway 2.

Happy #Thursday afternoon from the #Northland! The first of two #winter #storm systems continues to push through the region, keeping #temperatures #cool and #winds strong off #LakeSuperior. Most of us are seeing #snow, but some #sleet and #freezing #rain is […] [Original post on newsie.social]

NORTHLAND FORECAST FOR APRIL 2, 2026: A strong #storm system will bring #snow #showers into the #Northland Wednesday night, along with strong #winds off #LakeSuperior. Thursday will see all snow showers in northeast #Minnesota, with #sleet and #freezingrain […] [Original post on newsie.social]

A strong storm system will bring snow showers through the Northland tonight and Thursday, with sleet and freezing rain mixing in across northwest Wisconsin and the Upper Peninsula. Winds will be strong off Lake Superior. VIDEO/DISCUSSION: fox21online.com/weather

NORTHLAND FORECAST FOR MARCH 5, 2025: A strengthening Colorado low pushing through the #GreatLakes region will bring heavy #snow to the southern half of the #Northland Tuesday night and Wednesday. #Snowfall totals of over 12 inches will be likely along the #SouthShore of #LakeSuperior. #Winds […]

Happy #Monday afternoon from the #Northland! I'm back at the station after a nice vacation, and still seeing some #airquality issues with #wildfire #smoke lingering around the #GreatLakes. Otherwise, we're just watching the #radar for some #showers as […] [Original post on newsie.social]

NORTHLAND FORECAST FOR JANUARY 9, 2026: A #storm system passing through the #GreatLakes will bring #snow #showers to just the #UpperPeninsula and northwest #Wisconsin regions of the #Northland Thursday night. Everyone will see #breezy winds with lows in the teens and low 20s. Friday will see […]

SOUTH SHORE HEADS UP: As for the traditional #lake #effect #snow effect, the #SouthShore of #LakeSuperior is likely to see a multi-day event start up Thursday. Some locations in the #UpperPeninsula could see over 12 inches of #snowfall through this weekend. #wxtooter #weather #wx #UPwx #WIwx

HOW WINDY & SNOWY WAS THIS WEEK: Wednesday was #windy across the #Northland. Big #storms will also bring the #gales along #LakeSuperior, but It isn't often that we see gusts over 40 mph away from the lake. It's also been #snowy in some spots this week. The […] [Original post on newsie.social]

4:45 P.M. RADAR UPDATE: The heavy #snowfall has reached the Arrowhead and North Shore regions of the #Northland. It has exited the Twin Ports. #Winds remain strong, with highest #gusts in Cook and Lake counties. #Blizzard Warning remains in effect along the […] [Original post on newsie.social]

NORTHLAND FORECAST FOR NOVEMBER 27, 2025: The first #winter #storm of the season in the #Northland will keep #snow #showers ongoing along the southern shoreline of #LakeSuperior Wednesday night. #Winds remain strong with low #temperatures in the teens. Thursday will see the snow move out and a […]

NORTHLAND FORECAST FOR NOVEMBER 26, 2025: A #winter #storm system brings heavy #snowfall and strong #winds through the #Northland Tuesday night. #Blizzard like conditions continue along the southern shoreline of #LakeSuperior Wednesday due to #lakeeffect #snow #showers. Everyone else will see […]

Happy #Tuesday afternoon from the #Northland! A passing #clipper system continues to bring some of us #snow #showers through the rest of today. The focus will be around #LakeSuperior. It is a #warmer day compared to yesterday, but it will #cool down again […] [Original post on newsie.social]

Happy #Sunday afternoon from the #Northland! The worst of the #arctic #cold is passed us. We are going to see a #warm up the next several days. Some light #snow #showers are passing through the rest of today, mainly north of Highway 2 and around #LakeSuperior […] [Original post on newsie.social]

FOG AND FREEZING DRIZZLE: The #fog coming from #LakeSuperior is covering more areas of the #Northland as we go into Wednesday night. It will persist through Thursday morning. #Temperatures are also below #freezing, so the #drizzle could make surfaces and […] [Original post on newsie.social]
